frameworks.name: functional neuroimaging meta-analysis
frameworks.one_line: synthesizes neuroimaging activations to characterize angular gyrus functions

frameworks.name: cross-modal hub
frameworks.one_line: AG as convergence zone integrating multisensory information into concepts

core_question: What are the multiple functional roles and anatomical subdivisions of the angular gyrus?
key_assumptions: consistent task-evoked activations index functional involvement
AG processes concepts rather than percepts at perception-action interface
key_findings: AG consistently activates across semantic processing, reading, number, memory retrieval, attention, reasoning, and social cognition tasks
evidence supports multiple anatomical subdivisions within AG
AG involvement spans default mode network and spatial cognition
interpretations: AG functions as a cross-modal hub integrating multisensory information to comprehend events and reorient attention
AG processes concepts rather than percepts when interfacing perception, recognition, and action
